title: Bringing Pets to the Netherlands
metaTitle: Bringing Pets to the Netherlands: Rules, Documents & Travel Tips
subtitle: What expats often consider when relocating with dogs or cats.
description: Moving to the Netherlands with a dog or cat? Learn about pet import rules, documents, vaccinations, airline travel, housing, costs, and arrival planning.

image.alt: Expat with dog and luggage by a canal in the Netherlands; suitcase and pet carrier visible.

heroCta.title: Planning your move with pets?
heroCta.supportingText: Moving with a dog or cat usually means extra planning for microchips, vaccinations, veterinary documents, airline rules, housing, and early arrival logistics. Use the checklist and 90-day planner to organize the move before and after arrival.

quickAnswers.label: Who this guide is for
quickAnswers.value: Expats relocating to the Netherlands with dogs or cats, whether moving from another EU country or from outside the EU.

quickAnswers.label: Common pet relocation topics
quickAnswers.value: Documents, microchips, rabies vaccination, airline rules, pet-friendly housing, arrival steps, and settling in.

sections.heading: Overview
body: Bringing pets to the Netherlands often adds a separate layer of planning to the move. In addition to your own relocation steps, you may need to coordinate microchip records, vaccinations, veterinary paperwork, airline rules, travel crates, housing restrictions, and local veterinary setup after arrival.
This page gives a practical overview of what expats often need to think about before travel, during transport, and in the first days or weeks after arriving in the Netherlands with a pet.

links.label: Documents needed to move to the Netherlands
Cost of moving to the Netherlands

sections.heading: Relocating to the Netherlands with pets
body: Many expats bring dogs or cats when relocating to the Netherlands. Pet travel often needs to be planned earlier than the rest of the move, because documentation and timing usually have to line up in a specific order.
The exact requirements depend on the country of origin, pet type, airline, and travel route. Housing suitability also matters, because not all rentals allow pets.

sections.heading: Pet import rules for the Netherlands
body: Pets entering the Netherlands usually need to meet EU import conditions. At a practical level, owners often need to confirm identification, vaccination status, veterinary certification, and the correct travel route before departure.

Requirements often differ depending on whether you are travelling from an EU country or from outside the EU, the country’s risk status, pet species, airline and route, and whether the animal travels in the cabin, hold, or cargo. Confirm current rules before travel.

links.label: Documents needed to move to the Netherlands

sections.heading: Pet documents often required
body: Pet relocation often requires veterinary documentation and identification records that must be prepared before travel. Exact document requirements can vary depending on where the pet is coming from and how it will travel.
Some documents are only valid for a limited period before travel, so timing matters as much as document availability.
bullets: Airline booking confirmation for pet travel
Owner contact details linked to identification records
Import or route-specific documents if required

callout.title: Timing matters
callout.text: Some documents are only valid for a limited period before travel, so timing matters as much as document availability.

ctaBlock.title: Prepare pet travel documents early
ctaBlock.supportingText: Use the moving checklist to track pet identification, vaccinations, veterinary paperwork, and travel bookings before departure.

sections.heading: EU pet passport
body: Within the EU, pets often travel with an EU pet passport that records key identification and vaccination details. It is commonly used for repeat travel within the EU once the pet is already documented in that system.

An EU pet passport is not the same as every pre-entry travel certificate. Pets entering from outside the EU may initially need another type of veterinary health document before an EU pet passport becomes relevant.
bullets: Not always the initial entry document for non-EU moves

sections.heading: Rabies vaccination rules
body: Rabies vaccination is one of the most important parts of pet travel planning. In many cases, the pet must first be microchipped, then vaccinated, and then wait a required period before travel.
A waiting period often applies after vaccination; exact rules depend on the route and country of origin. Confirm current requirements before booking travel. Late planning can delay departure.

links.label: Documents needed to move to the Netherlands

sections.heading: Airline and travel considerations
body: Transporting pets internationally often involves airline rules, crate standards, seasonal limitations, and route-specific constraints. Not every airline accepts pets the same way, and not every route has the same handling options.
bullets: Cabin vs checked vs cargo handling

rows: Depends on pet size and airline rules
Airlines often require approved crate dimensions
Some routes limit pet travel in hot or cold conditions
Can add handling and timing complexity

services.indicativeCost: e.g. €2,000–€5,000+ for full door-to-door; get a quote for your route.

services.name: PetAir UK

services.indicativeCost: e.g. €800–€2,500+ for assisted moves; quote for your route.

sections.heading: Pet relocation costs
body: Moving with a pet can add meaningful extra costs to your relocation budget. The total depends on pet size, airline route, veterinary requirements, whether you use a relocation service, and whether special crates or transit handling are needed.
The table below gives planning ranges only. Confirm current provider pricing for your situation.

rows: Depends on vet and document requirements
Depends on size and airline requirements
Depends on route, airline, and pet size

callout.title: Plan early to reduce cost
callout.text: Pet relocation is often cheapest when planned early. Last-minute travel changes or reissued documents can increase costs quickly.

links.label: Cost of moving to the Netherlands

sections.heading: Finding pet-friendly housing
body: Housing is often one of the biggest practical issues when moving with pets. Some landlords or buildings allow pets, while others limit them or apply extra conditions.
Pet-friendly housing can take longer to find in competitive rental markets, so it helps to start early.
bullets: Think about nearby walking areas or outdoor access
Consider temporary housing if you need time to find a pet-friendly long-term rental
Keep pet details ready for housing discussions

ctaBlock.title: Plan pet travel and housing together
ctaBlock.supportingText: Use the checklist to coordinate airline planning, pet-friendly housing, and your first-week setup in the Netherlands.

sections.heading: Arrival with pets in the Netherlands
body: After arrival, pets may need immediate practical follow-up such as confirming identification records, checking health status after travel, and arranging local veterinary care.
Arrival procedures can vary depending on route, airline, and port of entry.

services.name: AniCura Netherlands
services.description: Veterinary clinic network in the Netherlands useful for post-arrival registration and routine care.
services.indicativeCost: e.g. €50–€150 for check-up; consultation fees vary by clinic.

services.name: Petplan Netherlands
services.description: Pet insurance provider in the Netherlands for ongoing veterinary cost planning after arrival.
services.indicativeCost: e.g. €15–€40/month depending on breed and coverage.

services.name: Zooplus Netherlands
services.description: Online pet supplies retailer useful for crates, food, bedding, and settling items.
services.indicativeCost: e.g. €80–€300 for crates and bedding; product prices online.

sections.heading: Helping pets settle after relocation
body: Relocation can be stressful for pets, especially after long travel or a major environment change. A calm routine can help dogs and cats adjust more smoothly.
bullets: Rebuild feeding and walking routines quickly
Identify nearby parks or walking routes
Keep familiar bedding or toys available
Watch behavior and appetite after travel

sections.heading: Practical pet relocation checklist
body: Use this as a planning aid alongside your wider move timeline.
bullets: Confirm whether your travel route changes document requirements
Check microchip and vaccination status early
Prepare veterinary documents within the correct timeframe
Research airline policies before booking flights
Buy the correct crate if needed
Plan first-week veterinary setup after arrival
Add pet steps to your wider move timeline

callout.title: Common mistake
callout.text: Treating pet travel as a last-week task. Many issues come from timing, not just missing paperwork.

faq.q: Can I bring my dog to the Netherlands?
faq.a: Yes, in most cases. Dogs can usually enter the Netherlands if they meet EU import conditions: microchip, rabies vaccination, and the correct veterinary documentation. Exact rules depend on your country of origin and travel route—confirm current requirements before booking.

faq.q: Can I bring my cat to the Netherlands?
faq.a: Yes, in most cases. Cats are typically subject to the same EU pet import framework as dogs: identification, rabies vaccination, and health documentation. Requirements can vary by origin country and airline; confirm before travel.

faq.q: Do pets need a microchip to enter the Netherlands?
faq.a: In most cases, yes. Microchip identification is commonly required for international pet travel into the EU. The microchip is usually implanted before or at the same time as the rabies vaccination. Confirm current rules for your route.

faq.q: Is rabies vaccination required for pets?
faq.a: In most cases, yes. Rabies vaccination is one of the main requirements for bringing pets into the Netherlands. There is often a waiting period after vaccination before travel is allowed. Exact timing depends on origin country and route—check current requirements.

faq.q: Can pets travel in the airplane cabin?
faq.a: It depends on the airline and pet size. Some airlines allow small dogs and cats in the cabin in an approved carrier; others only allow pets in the hold or as cargo. Rules vary by airline, route, and animal size—confirm before booking.

faq.q: Is quarantine required for pets entering the Netherlands?
faq.a: For many routes, no—pets meeting EU requirements often do not face quarantine. Rules depend on origin country and risk status. Confirm current requirements with official sources before travel.

faq.q: Are pets allowed in rental housing in the Netherlands?
faq.a: It depends on the landlord and building. Some rentals allow pets; others prohibit them or set conditions. Always confirm pet policy before signing a lease. Pet-friendly housing can take longer to find in competitive markets.

faq.q: Can expats bring pets to the Netherlands?
faq.a: Many expats relocate with pets. Preparation and documentation are usually required—microchip, vaccinations, veterinary paperwork, and sometimes airline or route-specific steps. Plan early and confirm current rules for your situation.
